posts.text: If memory is part of a computer than yes, the context is part of the LLM. The LLM generates content that gets fed back into it. You asked what part of the LLM "resembles memory", it's the part that the LLM literally gets to write into and persist.

reply_context.text: The way I tend to frame it is that it's in "opposition to craftsmanship" in thee exact same way as owning a 3D printer or CNC machine. For some people it's just a shortcut, but for others (esp. who already had high in-domain skill before the new toy) it opens doors to new forms of expression.

posts.text: The way I tend to frame it is that it's in "opposition to craftsmanship" in thee exact same way as owning a 3D printer or CNC machine. reply_context.text: my gut says that AI does empower a certain kind of abstracted higher level artistry, but I feel like it's in direct opposition with craftsmanship, and to some extent creativity too, in the sense that it often bypasses the need to create or even being creative

posts.text: PS: this rule of thumb has been making the rounds since at least 1988. Aged like wine.

reply_context.text: The rule of thumb is "If it's easy for humans, it might be deceptively hard for AI. If it's hard for humans, it might be deceptively easy for AI." Simple arithmetic is the kind of thing you'd think it ought to be good at, but generative brains are layers of approximations.
